questions.question: Anita, who turned 58 last Tuesday (26/06/2016), was born on which day of the week?
questions.explanation: Anita turned 58 on 26/06/2016, which was a Tuesday. Hence, she was born on 26/06/1958. Number of years in between is 58. Number of Leap years in between is 1960, 1964, 1968 .. 2016 Hence 15 Leap years in between. Now every Leap year brings forth 2 days change, and every normal years brings forth 1 day change in the birth day of the week. Total odd days = Remainder of (58 + 15)/7 = Remainder of 73/7 = 3 Hence Anita's birth day of the week must have been 3 days behind Tuesday, which must have been a Saturday

questions.explanation: Apart from 4, others are cities in Uttar Pradesh.

questions.question: Complete the series: 7, 13, 25, 49, 97, ?
questions.explanation: The difference between consecutive numbers is 6x20, 6x21, 6x22, 6x23, 6x24

questions.question: In the following question, select the related number from the given alternatives. 49: 245 ∷ 37 : ?
In the following question, select the related word from the given alternatives. Sugarcane: ? ∷ Cumin: Rabi
questions.explanation: As Cumin is Rabi crop, Sugarcane is Kharif crop.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: All others except option (c) have same meaning i.e. opening, while "obstruction" means blockage or closed path.

questions.question: In the following question, which one set of letters when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? a b _ c d _ _ b c _ a b b _ d a _ b c d
questions.explanation: a b b c d/ a b b c d/ a b b c d/ a b b c d

questions.question: The series is given, with one term missing. Choose amongst the given responses and complete the series. HDFG, GEEH, FFDI, EGCJ, __.
questions.explanation: 1st letter of each term is in reverse alphabetical order. H, G, F, E, D 2nd letter of each term is in alphabetical order. D, E, F, G, H 3rd letter of each term is in reverse alphabetical order. F, E, D, C, B 4th letter of each term is in alphabetical order. G, H, I, J, K

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number pair from the given alternatives.

In the following question, a word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in the two given matrices. You have to identify the correct set for the word "INWARD" Matrix I 01234 0DOBAI 1OBAID 2BAIDO 3AIDOB 4IDOBA Matrix II 56789 5WNRML 6NRMLW 7RMLWN 8MLWNR 9LWNRM
questions.explanation: From matrix I, I can be coded as 04, 13, 22, 31 or 40 From matrix II, N can be coded as 56, 65, 79, 88 or 97. From matrix II, W can be coded as 55, 69, 78, 87 or 96. From matrix I, A can be coded as 03, 12, 21, 30 or 44. From matrix II, R can be coded as 57, 66, 75, 89 or 98 From matrix I, D can be coded as 00, 14, 23, 32 or 41. Only the sequence 13, 56, 55, 03, 98, 14 has the letter codes in the correct order.

questions.question: Find the missing number from the given responses.
EGJ : JLO : ? : MOR.
questions.explanation: The correct sequence should have one letter between the first and second letters, two letters between the second and third letters. Example: E(F)G(H,I)J and also the last letter of the first word should be the first letter of the second word. G(H)I(J,K)L.

questions.question: Which one of the answer figures shall complete the given question figure. A. B. C. D.
A piece of paper is folded and cut as shown below in the question figures, from the given answer figures, indicate how it will appear when opened. A. B. C. D.
questions.explanation: The option (2) is correct.

questions.question: In a certain language, MIGRATION is written as HHNQCSPMJ. How can be ENVIRONMENT written in that code?
questions.explanation: In the code, MIGRATION → MIGR-A-TION → letters before the middle letter are arranged in alphabetical order while letters after the middle letter are arranged in reverse alphabetical order i.e. GIMR-A-TONI. Then, G I M R A T O N I +1 -1 +1 -1 +2 -1 +1 -1 +1 H H N Q C S P M J So, ENVIRONMENT → ENVIR-O-NMENT → EINRV-O-TNNME → FHOQW-Q-SOMND

questions.question: Piyush's house is to the north-west of Shivam's house and Rajeev's house is to the north-east of Shivam's house. Shivam's house is to the west of Ravi's house and to the north-west of Nandan's house. Whose house is situated at the center of the location among all of the friend's residence?
questions.explanation: Shivam is situated at the center of the location among all of the friend's residence.

questions.question: See these figures and select the number opposite to the number as asked in each of the question. Which number is on the opposite surface of number 3?
questions.explanation: Figures (i), (iii) and (iv) show that numbers 6, 1, 5, 2 appear on the adjacent surfaces to the number 3, Hence, 4 will be opposite to number 3.

questions.question: In the given figure, which letter represents the players who play only Golf and Football?
questions.explanation: G comes within triangle and square but not within circle, which represents cricket.

questions.question: From the given alternative words, select the one which can be formed using the letters of the given word. REPRESENTATIVE
questions.explanation: The word Repeat only formed by using the letters of the given word.

questions.question: From the given answer figures, select the one in which question figure is hidden/embedded. A. B. C. D.
questions.explanation: Option 2 and option 4 does not have that diagonal line, Option 3 does not have that horizontal line. Hence Option 1 is the correct answer.

questions.question: If in a certain way, 9 + 7 = 386 and 8 + 7 = 169, then find 9 + 3 ?
If '+' is represented by '÷', '-' is represented by 'x', 'x' is represented by '+' and '÷' is represented by '-', then what will be the value of 11 ÷ 15 + 81 - 27 x 18?
questions.explanation: Generally when we write '+' means '÷', and expression is '6 + 4', it would mean '6 ÷ 4'. When we are writing '+' is represented by '÷', it means that when there is '÷' in the expression, it is representing '+' and so '6 ÷ 4' becomes '6 + 4'. The given expression becomes 11 + 15 x 81 ÷ 27 - 18 = 11 + 15 x 3 - 18 = 11+45-18 = 38

questions.question: In each of the following questions, choose the correct mirror image of the figure from amongst the four alternatives. Problem figure Answer figures
questions.explanation: After reflection, the arrow will keep pointing top but its head will be near the left edge and its tail near the right edge. In the bottom figure, the open line will be near the left edge and vertex of the triangle near the right edge.

questions.question: Among five persons Sunny, Swati, Somya, Surbhi and Sapna; Swati was elder to Somya but younger to Sunny. Surbhi was elder to Sunny who was elder to Sapna who was not the youngest. Who among them was the youngest?
questions.explanation: Swati was elder to Somya but younger to Sunny Sunny> Swati> Somya Surbhi was elder to Sunny who was elder to Sapna who was not the youngest Surbhi> Sunny> Swati/Sapna> Somya Thus, Somya was the youngest

questions.question: Each question given below consists of a statement, followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the arguments is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument. Statement: Should luxury hotels be banned in India? Arguments: I. Yes. They are places from where international criminals operate. II. No. Affluent foreign tourists will have no place to stay.
options: If only argument I is strong.
If only argument II is strong.
If either I or II is strong.
If neither I nor II is strong.
questions.explanation: The luxury hotels are a mark of country's standard and a place for staying for the affluent foreign tourists. So, argument II holds. Argument I is not a strong reason because ban on hotel is not a way to do away with the activities of international criminals.
